title: Traveling with Kyle - Guyana Part 3, Brazil

transcript.text: Pay you to get all the way there but I forgot to get my deposit money back from the hotel so he's
Like oh yeah no problem so he stops and I jump out of the window and all the people in the bus
Were like yeah no worries and they just hung hung out there for like 10 minutes while I went out
And got my my deposit money back yeah that was that was cool I'm really patient so then got in
The bus and drove that down and there are these two American kids there who I guess were part of
Some program some college funded program or they were there helping people build schools or
Something so I stayed with them in their house and like partied with them that was fun one night and
Then took the bus back into Georgetown and walked around there for a day and then decided to take
A bus back to a truck back into the original town on the south far south border of oh no was over I
Went over on that one first time that happened yeah I decided to take the bus back um and of course I
Didn't go back I didn't go back to see if my friend was there he wouldn't have had money probably would
Have tried to get me to pay for him to go back south again so I just jumped on booked a truck it was
Like a big pickup but it had these tires that weren't big enough it didn't have monster truck tires
Which you needed if it was gonna do that trip the size of that truck but uh I was hanging out at the
Truck place with some guy who was a white guy um a ratty looking but he said he was uh he was uh he
Owned the diamond mind there and uh he said he was like the third richest family in the whole country or
Something and uh he was going to fly back to that town that day it was like 150 bucks or something
Like that I can't remember how much it was but um he uh he had a girlfriend there with him he said she
Was a prostitute but she was his girlfriend um and he said like one time he went to las vegas and
Spent like a hundred thousand dollars one night just gambling but uh he was cool and he was like
He said he was gonna go get a joint and come back and smoke it with me or give it to me or something
So he took off but he never came back so then I got on the bus the pickup and I shared it with these
Brazilian guys but I didn't talk to them all the time they were just they weren't talking to themselves
Either but we drove down the road and uh just south of Georgetown there are these um lots of these
Factory like mining refineries like they do a lot of mining in in Guyana they mine for diamonds like
This guy was telling me and then they mine for like copper or something like that so I saw this
Smokestack smoking up and um Georgetown the uh you know it's really spread out also the town itself
Is really small but there's lots of little satellite towns that's basically the whole population of the
Country is right there along the coast there um and I saw on the news like there was actually a
Head-on collision on that road and like all the people in the like a car died but I didn't really
Notice our driver going too fast but we got to uh the checkpoint area before you go in and out of
The jungle you have to like stamp your passport or something I don't know why maybe I don't know why
They do that but um it was at nighttime and we stopped and there's this cop there this older black guy
But he was he was totally wasted drunk like who he was so he could like you know he was like slurring
His speech and he had this huge shotgun with him and for some reason he decided he didn't like
The driver of my truck of my pickup there was a driver and then he had a friend with him like his
Assistant or something I don't know what it was and then this big black lady and then me and I was
Sitting in the front seat like next to them and then I guess later on I got in the back with the
Brazilians just on the back of the pickup there's like three of those Brazilians but I'm here they're
Like two black ladies but but like of course they're black everybody's black there but anyways uh
This guy like decided he didn't like the truck driver the pickup driver so he would refuse to
Stamp anybody's passport and he was just sitting there like mumbling to himself and then he'd wave
The gun around saying he'd like shoot us or something and then he was trying to accuse the
Truck driver being drunk like you're drunk I'm not gonna let you pass and but the truck driver he was
Like really calm he was just kind of standing there like okay uh I think you could maybe stamp my best
My uh stamp my booklet maybe like what you're drunk you're a bad person I'm gonna shoot you
So we were there like a half hour until he finally got bored with himself and decided to stamp us all
In and uh we got in the pickup and kept going and we kept getting we crossed the river again but then
We kept getting stuck um I don't know why he had the small tires but it was a really big truck it would
Have done great if it had big tires but it didn't so we had to keep getting pulled out towed out by
Bedford's and other people and pickups the smaller pickups with bigger tires um so we did we and
There were our trip back involved a lot of us pushing the truck and sitting around waiting
Um so that ended up I thought it would take long be quicker than the Bedford trip but uh it wasn't it
Was just as long another 24-hour journey and then we stayed we went got some um like in the in the
In the plains area again we uh there was some guy I think it was like an American guy who owned it
It was like it was just a way station type of a place bar and the pickup driver bought me a beer I
Don't know why but uh it was cool and uh and then we drove down south and I went back to the original
Town I bought some weed off of uh the friends of my friend who introduced me to him and I asked him
If he'd come back and he hadn't come back yet so I hung out with them for a night and uh then came back into uh
Uh Brazil and went down and stayed a couple of nights or a night in the northernmost Brazil town before you go into
Venezuela I originally wanted to go into Venezuela but I didn't have any money I had to beeline it to Santiago
And tell him like teach English or something so uh I stayed there a night um saw a couple of movies
And then took the bus down into Manaus and stayed in Manaus a couple days because I had to wait like
Three or four days for the bus or the boat to leave so uh I did the tour of their they have this big opera house
There that was really pretty up on the top of a hill overlooking um that you can overlook the river
And there's a big you know kind of plaza area just to the east of it and it was really it's really old
Like 150 years old or something and it was really nice all these old wooden chairs and wooden floors and
Floors and everything and uh and then saw some movies over in the edge of town it was like a half hour bus
Ride for some reason I didn't go to the discos and I just didn't want to spend money extra money drinking
Because I have a bad habit of when I go drinking I drink way too much and I get way too drunk and I
Spend way too much money to get myself in trouble so I just hung out during the day and uh did that
And then I would I think I was staying on the boat though the whole time that was cool then the boat left
And uh we took the boat down and where the two rivers appear we took a right and went down the southern river
I can't remember what it's called but it wasn't as wide this time it was maybe a hundred meters wide
And it was the same kind of boat just trudging down and I had a uh a hammock only this one wasn't as crowded
As the first one and uh I brought myself a big old bottle of cognac cognac and it was full I put it down
Below my hammock and I went and hung out on the roof there for like an hour with some like German girl or
Something and there were a bunch of Israeli guys and then I came down I looked at my cognac it was almost
Empty so I picked it up I look at it and then I look over at the guy next to me this Brazilian guy
And he's hanging his hammock and he goes so that was like a quintessential moment for me for Brazil
Like that's basically what describes the Brazilian was just they're just like totally like totally laid
Back uh you know he felt like drinking my cognac so he did it and he didn't see anything wrong with it
Whatever and these but I mean they they do share too like the ones on the other boat ride were sharing
Me their alcohol while they were hanging out and drinking
